danger_signals.visual_form: Red/orange animated flames in grid cells, spreading animation
Dark red or black overlay on affected city blocks
danger_signals.meaning: High crime rate reducing property values and safety

danger_signals.visual_form: Gray/brown haze overlays above industrial zones
Flashing or animated road segments in red
danger_signals.meaning: Road network overwhelmed, economic efficiency reduced

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened grid sections with no visible lighting effects
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of electrical service affecting zone functionality

danger_signals.visual_form: Weather overlays (tornado icon, flood water animation)
danger_signals.meaning: Natural disaster events destroying infrastructure and population

safety_signals.visual_form: Light green colored grid cells with house sprite variations
Blue grid cells with storefront/building sprites
Yellow grid cells with factory sprites
Power lines with no breaks, connected to power plant
Continuous road paths connecting zones, no gaps
Green tree sprites visible in city and park areas

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: All constructed zones, road network, public services

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Creature that destroys zones on contact
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears as random destructive event, leaves damage pattern

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large clusters of blue commercial and yellow industrial zones, dense housing, growing skyline
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zone growth animation, increased traffic flow, population increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blue/yellow zones shrink, turn dark or disappear, abandoned structures appear, green residential fades
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zones depopulate, traffic decreases, construction stops

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark red/black overlays on residential zones, visible crime sprite or warning indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population flight from affected areas, reduced zone development

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gray/brown atmospheric haze grows across map, visible over industrial clusters and downwind areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Health indicators drop, residential zones decline, demand shifts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power lines show broken segments in red, zones darken, power plant sprite dims or shows damage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Affected zones stop functioning, industry halts, services unavailable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated destruction sequence (fire spread, earthquake cracking, flood water, tornado spiral), affected zones blacken and clear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Immediate zone loss, emergency services deploy, rebuilding phase begins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Balanced mix of green/blue/yellow zones, clear road network, visible parks and trees, no overlays or dark areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Steady population growth, rising tax revenue, construction activity across city

environmental_storytelling.example: Green residential zones with house sprites tell story of habitation; sparse green with red overlay tells story of decline and crime
Visible power lines and connected power plant communicate functional electrical system; broken/disconnected lines show systemic failure
Gray haze intensity increases toward industrial zones and downwind, visually narrating pollution source and spread
Blackened/cleared zones left by fire, flood, or earthquake show permanent scars of disaster, requiring rebuilding effort
Building sprite density in zones communicates population density and economic vibrancy; sparse building sprites show stagnation
Moving vehicle sprites on roads and animated traffic congestion overlay show real-time transportation state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite density increases, more varied house types appear, green coloration brightens
Sprite density decreases, houses appear damaged or abandoned, green fades to gray
Blue coloration intense, storefront sprites multiply, building height varies
Blue fades, storefronts disappear, zone reverts to empty grid
Yellow intense, factory sprites prominent, smoke/pollution animation visible
Yellow fades, factory sprites disappear, pollution haze clears around area
Red/orange animated flame spreads to adjacent zones, consumes structures
Flame animation slows and stops, fire department sprites appear on scene
Road segments flash or animate red, vehicle sprites cluster densely
Roads appear normal gray, vehicle sprites move smoothly with spacing

notes: SimCity SNES uses a tile-based grid visualization system where world-state is communicated primarily through: (1) Zone color and sprite density indicating economic vitality; (2) Atmospheric overlays (haze, darkness) showing pollution and infrastructure failure; (3) Animated destruction sequences showing disaster impact; (4) Infrastructure visualization (power lines, roads) showing system connectivity. The game employs a largely positive affordance system where green=growth and darker colors=danger. Behavioral tells are animated and real-time, giving players continuous feedback about city conditions. No faction conflict system exists—all danger comes from natural systems (crime, disasters, pollution) rather than opposing factions.
